# Vector Index Visual Explorer

## Project summary

This is a static web app that visually explains how vector index types work. It runs entirely in the browser with no server or build step. Each page is a self-contained interactive demo where users can edit vector values and see live results.

## Index types covered

- FLAT: Scans every stored vector. Exact results. Slow at scale.
- IVF_FLAT: Divides vectors into clusters. Searches only the nearest cluster.
- IVF_PQ: Compresses vectors into short codes using product quantization. Trades accuracy for memory.
- IVF_SQ8: Converts 32-bit floats to 8-bit integers. 75% memory reduction with minimal accuracy loss.
- HNSW: Builds a navigable graph. Hops node to node to find the nearest neighbor fast.
- DiskANN: Keeps frequently accessed vectors in RAM, rest on disk. Handles large datasets with small memory.

## Tech stack

- HTML, CSS (Tailwind CDN), Vanilla JavaScript
- No frameworks, no build tools, no dependencies to install

## File list

- index.html: Home page
- flat.html, ivf-flat.html, ivf-pq.html, ivf-sq8.html, hnsw.html, diskann.html: One page per index type
- script.js: Shared cosine similarity function and input helpers

## Similarity formula

cosine_similarity(A, B) = dot_product(A, B) / (magnitude(A) * magnitude(B))

## Interaction model

Every input on every page is editable. Results recompute on every keystroke. No submit button needed.

## Intended audience

Beginners learning vector search concepts. Applicable to any vector database platform.
